domains.name: Domain 2: Configuration Management and Infrastructure as Code
Domain 4: Monitoring, Logging, and Incident Response

questions.text: A company deployed AWS resources with a CloudFormation template that listed only properties required to create each resource. Optional settings used service defaults, then operators changed those defaults outside the stack. That caused drift from the template. The team wants every property value maintained in the template, wants to detect out-of-band changes, and wants the stack restored to the template definition. Which solution meets these requirements with the LEAST implementation effort?

options.text: Update the template to set every property (including former defaults). Create a Lambda function that detects stack drift and updates the stack from the template. Schedule it daily with Amazon EventBridge.
Create a Lambda function that detects drift on the existing stack and updates the stack using the current template. Invoke it on a daily schedule with Amazon EventBridge.

Update the template to set every property (including former defaults). Use the cloudformation-stack-drift-detection-check AWS Config rule with automatic remediation via the AWS-UpdateCloudFormationStack Systems Manager Automation runbook.
Use the cloudformation-stack-drift-detection-check AWS Config rule with automatic remediation via the AWS-UpdateCloudFormationStack Systems Manager Automation runbook only.

questions.explanation: Scheduled DetectStackDrift plus redeploying the known template is the lowest-effort path. Fully enumerating every property and rolling out org-wide Config remediation is heavier.

questions.text: An application on Amazon EC2 instances in multiple Availability Zones pulls messages from an Amazon SQS queue. During bursts, backlog grows while average CPU on the Auto Scaling group stays low. Target tracking on average CPU does not scale out quickly enough. Which solution resolves this in the MOST reliable way?

options.text: Publish a custom Amazon CloudWatch metric: visible SQS messages divided by in-service instances. Scale the Auto Scaling group on that metric against an acceptable target.
Place an Application Load Balancer in front of the group and scale on ALBRequestCountPerTarget.
Change the target tracking policy to use the SQS NumberOfMessagesReceived metric instead of CPU.
Lower the CPU target percentage and shorten the warm-up period on the existing policy.

questions.explanation: Queue depth per running instance reflects worker backlog; CPU is a weak signal for queue consumers.

questions.text: A DevOps engineer manages multiple accounts in AWS Organizations and must monitor selected service quotas daily in every account. Email alerts are required when usage reaches 80% of a quota. Which solution meets these requirements?

options.text: Enable trusted access between Service Quotas and Organizations. Create a CloudWatch alarm per needed service at 80% of quota utilization. Send Amazon SNS notifications when the alarm enters ALARM.
Deploy a Lambda function in each account that calls DescribeTrustedAdvisorCheckResult and opens support cases for WARN quota checks. Schedule it daily from the management account over EventBridge.
Enable Service Quotas trusted access and create 80% CloudWatch alarms, but route ALARM events through EventBridge instead of SNS.
Refresh Trusted Advisor checks daily with Lambda and publish to SNS when checks report 80% usage.

questions.explanation: Service Quotas integrates with Organizations and exposes utilization metrics for CloudWatch alarms; SNS is the standard notification channel.

questions.text: An application runs on EC2 behind an Application Load Balancer in an Auto Scaling group. AWS CodeDeploy performs blue/green deployments. Operators read application logs on instances and manually tell CodeDeploy to roll back when error rates are too high. Which TWO steps automate rollback? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Create a CloudWatch alarm on the ALB HTTPCode_Target_4XX_Count metric above a threshold.
Configure the deployment group to monitor the alarm, fail the deployment, and roll back when the alarm enters ALARM.
Use a scheduled Lambda every hour to roll back if an alarm is in ALARM.
Publish to SNS on ALARM and configure CodeDeploy to subscribe to the topic for rollback.
Send application logs to CloudWatch Logs, add a metric filter for error lines, and alarm on the error rate.

questions.explanation: CodeDeploy can tie rollback to deployment alarms; log metric filters catch errors that never appear as ALB HTTP codes.

questions.text: A DevOps engineer launched Elastic Beanstalk with the EB CLI and added an .ebextensions file that sets the instance type to t3.medium. The environment picked up new environment variables but still runs t3.small instances. What is the most likely root cause?

options.text: Default service values cannot be overridden by .ebextensions.
Values passed through the Elastic Beanstalk CLI/API (recommended values) take precedence over .ebextensions for settings such as instance type.
Instance type cannot be configured in .ebextensions.
The launching IAM principal lacks permission for t3.medium.

questions.explanation: Precedence is direct environment/API settings, saved configuration, .ebextensions, then defaults. CLI launches can apply recommended values that win over extensions.

questions.text: Amazon API Gateway fronts Lambda for a REST API. Route 53 provides DNS. A new Lambda version is ready; 10% of production traffic must hit the new functions for one week with no URL change for clients. What is the MOST operationally efficient deployment?

options.text: Deploy a new API stage and custom domain; use a simple Route 53 record to split 10%/90% traffic.
Add a canary release on the existing production stage: 10% to the new Lambda integration and 90% to the current integration.
Create a second API and second Route 53 domain; use weighted routing 10%/90%.
Switch to Lambda proxy integration and route 10% of requests inside the function.

questions.explanation: API Gateway canary deployments on the same stage shift traffic without client-side hostname changes.

questions.text: JSON logs on EC2 include PII. A third party must troubleshoot application events without PII and without SSH to production. Which solution is MOST cost-effective?

options.text: Central syslog on EC2; AWS Batch copies filtered logs to Amazon S3 once per day.
CloudWatch agent to CloudWatch Logs; Lambda writes all events to an always-on OpenSearch cluster; share OpenSearch Dashboards with the vendor.
CloudWatch agent directly to Amazon Data Firehose; Lambda transformation drops non-event lines; deliver to S3.
CloudWatch agent to CloudWatch Logs; subscription filter and Lambda write only application events to Amazon S3; grant the vendor S3 access.

questions.explanation: Subscription filters can strip PII; S3 is cost-effective for episodic vendor review without always-on search infrastructure.

questions.text: AWS Organizations spans many accounts and Regions. The company needs automated detection and blocking when any S3 bucket (existing or new) becomes public via ACLs or bucket policies. Which solution meets this?

options.text: EventBridge global endpoints with replication to a security account; Lambda removes public access.
Organization CloudTrail to a security-account bucket; EventBridge rules on that bus for all accounts.
Enable S3 EventBridge notifications on every bucket for ACL and policy changes.
AWS Config rule in each account (or organization) for public ACLs/policies with automated remediation via a Systems Manager Automation runbook.

questions.explanation: Managed Config rules such as s3-bucket-level-public-access-prohibited detect public access; runbooks like AWS-DisableS3BucketPublicReadWrite remediate automatically.

questions.text: Two CodePipeline flows build the same Git branch: one deploys to development, one to UAT. UAT shows a defect that does not appear in development at the same code version. How should the DevOps engineer ensure identical artifacts and identical environments?

options.text: Lambda validates artifacts; reuse one CodeBuild project in both pipelines; CodeDeploy deploys each environment.
One pipeline builds to S3; S3 events invoke validation Lambda; two pipelines deploy from S3.
Shared CodeBuild with different buildspec overrides per environment via --buildspec-override.
Lambda compares artifact SHA-256 checksums across pipelines after the UAT build; both deployment stages use the same AWS CloudFormation template to provision environments.

questions.explanation: One CloudFormation template keeps environments equivalent; checksum validation proves both pipelines shipped the same build output.

questions.text: A SAM application has an API Gateway HTTP API and Lambda functions. Authentication must validate a custom HTTP header and token by calling a legacy service that is not OpenID Connect or SAML. Which mechanism should the team use?
questions.explanation: A Lambda authorizer calls your existing auth service and returns an IAM policy for allow or deny.

questions.text: A hybrid workload will use AWS CodeDeploy on on-premises servers. What is the MOST secure way to register targets and deploy with tag-based deployment groups?

options.text: IAM user access keys in a local credentials file; register instance IDs with the deployment group.
IAM user access keys in a file; tag servers; tag-based deployment group.
IAM role with STS AssumeRole; refresh credentials on a schedule; install the CodeDeploy agent; register on-premises instances; tag-based deployment group.
Same as (c) but register a list of instance IDs instead of tags.

questions.explanation: Temporary credentials from AssumeRole avoid long-lived keys; CodeDeploy deployment groups target tags.

questions.text: EC2 instances behind an ALB in an Auto Scaling group must register with an external auditing service before they process transactions. Which solution meets this?

options.text: Add a lifecycle hook to put new instances in Pending:Wait; run a registration script; complete the lifecycle action with CONTINUE or ABANDON.
User data bootstrap script registers the instance; return a non-zero exit code on failure.
EventBridge schedule every 5 minutes invokes Lambda to register any new instances.
EventBridge rule on EC2 launch invokes Lambda to register asynchronously.

questions.explanation: Lifecycle hooks block the Auto Scaling group until registration succeeds or the instance is abandoned.

questions.text: Legal documents live in Amazon S3. Users set LegalHold=true on some objects. The company must delete objects older than 90 days that are not on legal hold. Uploading apps cannot be changed to add tags on PUT. Which TWO steps meet the requirements? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Bucket policy denying PutObject unless s3:RequestObjectTag/LegalHold is true or false.
Lifecycle expiration after 90 days with no tag filter.
S3 event notification on object creation invokes Lambda to set LegalHold=false when the tag is missing.
Bucket policy denying DeleteObject when s3:ExistingObjectTag/LegalHold is true.
Lifecycle rule with filter LegalHold=false that expires objects older than 90 days.

questions.explanation: Lambda defaults the tag so lifecycle can filter; expiration with LegalHold=false skips held documents.

questions.text: Amazon ECS tasks enter STOPPED when an essential container exits with stoppedReason "Essential container in task exited". An SNS topic exists for email alerts. What should the engineer do next with the LEAST development effort?

options.text: Configure ECS cluster notification options for lastStatus STOPPED.
Create an EventBridge rule on aws.ecs for STOPPED tasks with that stoppedReason; target the SNS topic.
Lambda polls DescribeTasks on a schedule and publishes matching tasks to SNS.
Set notifications=true on essential containers in the task definition.

questions.explanation: EventBridge captures ECS task state changes natively; SNS delivers email without custom polling code.

questions.text: A web API uses API Gateway, Lambda, DynamoDB, and ElastiCache for Redis OSS. How should a DevOps engineer deploy this with the MOST operational efficiency?

options.text: Deploy all infrastructure with one AWS Serverless Application Model (AWS SAM) template.
Deploy everything with CloudFormation and custom resources for API, Lambda, and DynamoDB.
CloudFormation for ElastiCache; SAM for the remaining components.
CloudFormation template without the SAM transform.

questions.explanation: SAM extends CloudFormation for serverless workloads in a single maintainable template.

questions.text: Development teams create EC2 instances, but some hosts do not use security-approved AMIs. The DevOps team needs automatic discovery and termination of noncompliant instances. What should they implement?

options.text: Approved AWS Service Catalog products only.
SCP on an IAM group denying ec2:RunInstances unless the AMI is approved.
AWS Config rule detecting noncompliant instances with remediation that terminates them.

questions.explanation: Config continuously evaluates instances; remediation can terminate hosts that violate the AMI rule.

questions.text: EC2 instances behind an ALB drift because of launch-time differences and manual SSH changes. The company wants identical configuration at launch and over the instance lifetime. Which solution meets this?

options.text: Golden AMI per OS update; NewestInstance termination policy; disable console access; Systems Manager maintenance.
Use the default AWS AMI and patch in user data on each launch; OldestLaunchConfiguration policy.
Golden AMI; OldestLaunchConfiguration; double capacity then scale in; use AWS Config for emergency maintenance.
Golden AMI per update; OldestLaunchConfiguration termination; double capacity then return to original size; disable OS console access; use AWS Systems Manager for maintenance.

questions.explanation: Immutable AMIs plus replacing oldest instances removes drift; Systems Manager supports controlled changes without ad hoc SSH.

questions.text: A public REST API’s Lambda now requires a language field in the JSON body. Only one production Lambda function should be maintained. Legacy clients omit language. How should the DevOps team deploy?

options.text: New API integrated with the new Lambda; keep the old API and Lambda unchanged.
Integrate the existing API with the new Lambda and delete the old function immediately.
New API for new clients; existing API unchanged so legacy calls still omit language.
New API for clients that send language; add a mapping template on the existing API integration that injects "language": "English"; delete the old Lambda after cutover.

questions.explanation: Mapping templates add defaults on the legacy stage while a new API serves explicit language values.

questions.text: A stateless web tier on EC2 behind an ALB uses Amazon RDS for MySQL Multi-AZ. DR in a second Region must achieve RTO under 2 hours and RPO under 10 minutes, cost-effectively. Which DR design fits?

options.text: Elastic Beanstalk environment in the DR Region; swap environment URLs; promote a cross-Region read replica.
Maintain current AMIs for the web tier in the DR Region; cross-Region RDS read replica; on disaster launch a CloudFormation stack, promote the replica, update DNS to the new load balancer.
Hourly Lambda copies EBS and RDS snapshots to the DR Region; rebuild from snapshots on disaster.
Active web tier in both Regions; Aurora global database with manual promotion in DR.

questions.explanation: Cross-Region read replicas meet tight RPO; AMIs plus IaC in DR meet RTO without full active-active cost.

questions.text: Python applications use Git, AWS CodeBuild, and AWS CodePipeline. The team needs automatic source review to find defects before compile. Which solution meets this?

options.text: Associate the Git repo with Amazon CodeGuru Profiler and review pull requests.
Run CodeGuru Profiler in the CodeBuild prebuild phase.
Associate the Git repository with Amazon CodeGuru Reviewer.
Run CodeGuru Reviewer only in CodeBuild without associating the repository.

questions.explanation: CodeGuru Reviewer analyzes pull requests for defects and best practices in Python and Java.

questions.text: Operators want every optional CloudFormation property defined in code and drift corrected by re-applying the full template, using managed governance rather than a custom Lambda scheduler. What should they add?

options.text: DetectStackDrift Lambda on a cron only
Explicit properties in the template plus AWS Config drift detection with AWS-UpdateCloudFormationStack remediation

questions.explanation: Config’s CloudFormation drift check plus the standard remediation runbook updates stacks to match templates.

questions.text: During a blue/green CodeDeploy cutover, target 5xx rates from the load balancer are not rising, but application log errors are. Which signal should drive automated rollback?

options.text: Deployment group linked to a log-derived error metric alarm
Manual SNS email to operators only

questions.explanation: Application errors may appear only in logs; metric filters surface them to CodeDeploy alarms.

questions.text: Security needs daily detection of public S3 buckets across many accounts without writing custom drift Lambdas per account. Which managed capability applies?

options.text: Organization-wide AWS Config conformance pack with public access rules and remediation
CloudFront in front of all buckets

questions.explanation: Conformance packs roll Config rules and remediation to all accounts in the organization.

questions.text: Chaos testing should inject EC2 stop failures in a non-production environment with guardrails. Which AWS service is purpose-built?

options.text: AWS Fault Injection Simulator experiment templates

questions.explanation: FIS provides controlled fault injection experiments with safety controls.

questions.text: A company needs to orchestrate CI/CD with manual approvals. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort?

options.text: AWS CodePipeline with manual approval actions

questions.explanation: Manual approvals gate production.

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to run tests in isolated build environments. Which approach is MOST reliable?

options.text: AWS CodeBuild with privileged mode only when needed

questions.explanation: CodeBuild scales build fleet elastically.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must implement GitOps for EKS. Which design fits best?

options.text: Flux/Argo CD on EKS or CodePipeline to cluster

questions.explanation: GitOps reconciles cluster state from Git.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must sign and verify container images. Which design fits best?

options.text: Notation/Cosign with ECR and admission controls

questions.explanation: Image signing prevents tampered deploys.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must deploy Lambda with canary traffic shifting. Which design fits best?
questions.explanation: Canary releases limit blast radius.

questions.text: A company needs to store pipeline artifacts immutably. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort?

options.text: CodePipeline S3 artifact store with encryption

questions.explanation: Artifacts should be versioned and encrypted.

questions.text: During peak traffic, the platform team must trigger pipelines from Git webhooks securely. What should they do FIRST?
questions.explanation: Connections use OAuth to external Git.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to run compliance tests in pipeline. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution?

options.text: CloudFormation Guard or cfn-nag in CodeBuild

questions.explanation: Policy-as-code catches misconfigurations early.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would orchestrate CI/CD with manual approvals. Design B would run tests in isolated build environments. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?

options.text: AWS CodePipeline with manual approval actions
AWS CodeBuild with privileged mode only when needed

questions.explanation: Manual approvals gate production. Compare with AWS CodeBuild with privileged mode only when needed: CodeBuild scales build fleet elastically.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would run tests in isolated build environments. Design B would implement GitOps for EKS. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?

options.text: AWS CodeBuild with privileged mode only when needed
Flux/Argo CD on EKS or CodePipeline to cluster

questions.explanation: CodeBuild scales build fleet elastically. Compare with Flux/Argo CD on EKS or CodePipeline to cluster: GitOps reconciles cluster state from Git.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to define reusable infrastructure modules. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution?

options.text: AWS CDK constructs or CloudFormation modules

questions.explanation: Modules promote reuse and standards.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to detect configuration drift on servers. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution?

options.text: Systems Manager Compliance and AWS Config

questions.explanation: Compliance reports deviations.

questions.text: A company needs to manage Ansible/Chef at scale. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort?

options.text: AWS OpsWorks or SSM State Manager

questions.explanation: Configuration management enforces desired state.

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to import existing resources into IaC. Which approach is MOST reliable?
questions.explanation: Resource import brings legacy under management.

questions.text: During peak traffic, the platform team must use stack sets for multi-account rollout. What should they do FIRST?
questions.explanation: StackSets deploy stacks across accounts/OUs.

questions.text: A company needs to parameterize environments safely. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort?

options.text: SSM Parameter Store hierarchies per env
Prod params in dev account shared

questions.explanation: Hierarchical parameters separate environments.

questions.text: A company needs to validate templates before deploy. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort?
questions.explanation: Change sets preview modifications.

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to manage SAM transforms for serverless. Which solution is BEST?
questions.explanation: SAM simplifies Lambda/API definitions.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would define reusable infrastructure modules. Design B would detect configuration drift on servers. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?

options.text: AWS CDK constructs or CloudFormation modules
Systems Manager Compliance and AWS Config

questions.explanation: Modules promote reuse and standards. Compare with Systems Manager Compliance and AWS Config: Compliance reports deviations.

questions.text: A hybrid environment must support a goal to run chaos experiments safely without changing application code. What should you implement?
questions.explanation: FIS injects controlled failures.

questions.text: A hybrid environment must support a goal to define RTO/RPO in resilience hub without changing application code. What should you implement?
questions.explanation: Resilience Hub assesses apps against policies.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to multi-AZ RDS with automatic failover. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution?
questions.explanation: Multi-AZ fails over to standby.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to queue-based load leveling. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution?

options.text: SQS with Auto Scaling on queue depth

questions.explanation: Backpressure protects downstream systems.

questions.text: A company needs to regional failover for stateless tier. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort?

options.text: Route 53 failover + AMIs/Launch templates in DR Region

questions.explanation: Stateless tiers are easier to failover.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must use circuit breaker pattern in microservices. Which action is appropriate?

options.text: API Gateway throttling and client retries with jitter

questions.explanation: Circuit breakers prevent cascading outages.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must deploy ECS services across AZs. Which action is appropriate?
questions.explanation: Spread placement increases availability.

questions.text: A company needs to immutable infrastructure for EC2. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort?

options.text: Auto Scaling with golden AMI/Image Builder

questions.explanation: Immutable deploys replace instances instead of patching live.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would run chaos experiments safely. Design B would define RTO/RPO in resilience hub. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: FIS injects controlled failures. Compare with AWS Resilience Hub: Resilience Hub assesses apps against policies.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would define RTO/RPO in resilience hub. Design B would multi-AZ RDS with automatic failover. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Resilience Hub assesses apps against policies. Compare with Amazon RDS Multi-AZ: Multi-AZ fails over to standby.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would multi-AZ RDS with automatic failover. Design B would queue-based load leveling. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?

options.text: SQS with Auto Scaling on queue depth

questions.explanation: Multi-AZ fails over to standby. Compare with SQS with Auto Scaling on queue depth: Backpressure protects downstream systems.

questions.text: For cost and security, the team must correlate logs metrics and traces. What is the recommended approach?
questions.explanation: Unified observability speeds incident response.

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to create composite alarms to reduce noise. Which solution is BEST?
questions.explanation: Composite alarms combine child alarms logically.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must export logs to SIEM. Which design fits best?
questions.explanation: Streaming exports integrate with analytics/SIEM.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must audit privileged actions. Which design fits best?

options.text: CloudTrail management events and CloudTrail Lake

questions.explanation: CloudTrail Lake enables SQL analytics on events.

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to monitor CI/CD pipeline failures. Which solution is BEST?
questions.explanation: Event-driven notifications alert owners.

questions.text: During peak traffic, the platform team must track deployment frequency DORA metrics. What should they do FIRST?
questions.explanation: DORA metrics measure delivery performance.

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to use Synthetics for critical user journeys. Which approach is MOST reliable?
questions.explanation: Canaries detect outages before users.

questions.text: A company needs to alert on Auto Scaling failures. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort?
questions.explanation: Lifecycle hooks enable debugging launch issues.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would correlate logs metrics and traces. Design B would create composite alarms to reduce noise. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Unified observability speeds incident response. Compare with CloudWatch composite alarms: Composite alarms combine child alarms logically.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would create composite alarms to reduce noise. Design B would export logs to SIEM. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Composite alarms combine child alarms logically. Compare with CloudWatch subscription filters to Kinesis/Firehose/OpenSearch: Streaming exports integrate with analytics/SIEM.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would export logs to SIEM. Design B would audit privileged actions. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?

options.text: CloudTrail management events and CloudTrail Lake

questions.explanation: Streaming exports integrate with analytics/SIEM. Compare with CloudTrail management events and CloudTrail Lake: CloudTrail Lake enables SQL analytics on events.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would audit privileged actions. Design B would monitor CI/CD pipeline failures. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?

options.text: CloudTrail management events and CloudTrail Lake

questions.explanation: CloudTrail Lake enables SQL analytics on events. Compare with CodePipeline CloudWatch events and notifications: Event-driven notifications alert owners.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must route operational events to targets. Which design fits best?
questions.explanation: EventBridge reacts to AWS API and custom events.

questions.text: For cost and security, the team must manage incidents with runbooks. What is the recommended approach?
questions.explanation: Incident Manager coordinates response.

questions.text: A company needs to auto-remediate non-compliant resources. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort?

options.text: SSM Automation documents triggered by Config

questions.explanation: Automation executes approved remediation.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must isolate compromised EC2 quickly. Which action is appropriate?

options.text: Security group isolation runbook or NACL deny

questions.explanation: Isolation limits lateral movement.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to aggregate security findings for triage. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution?
questions.explanation: Security Hub prioritizes findings.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must capture forensics disk/memory. Which action is appropriate?

options.text: SSM Automation snapshot volumes with preservation

questions.explanation: Preserve evidence before remediation.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must notify on-call from alarms. Which action is appropriate?

options.text: SNS to AWS Chatbot or PagerDuty integration

questions.explanation: Integrations route alerts to on-call.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must document post-incident reviews. Which action is appropriate?

options.text: Runbooks and knowledge base in Incident Manager

questions.explanation: Post-incident reviews improve systems.

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to validate recovery procedures regularly. Which solution is BEST?
questions.explanation: Testing confirms RTO/RPO assumptions.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must use cell-based blast isolation for EKS. Which design fits best?
questions.explanation: Cells contain failures.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must protect against regional failure. Which action is appropriate?
questions.explanation: Multi-Region designs address Regional disasters.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must ensure idempotent deployments. Which action is appropriate?
questions.explanation: Idempotency makes retries safe.

questions.text: During peak traffic, the platform team must load test before peak events. What should they do FIRST?

options.text: Distributed Load Testing on AWS or third-party in VPC

questions.explanation: Load testing validates scaling policies.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to use SQS visibility timeout correctly. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution?
questions.explanation: Visibility timeout prevents duplicate work.

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to design EKS control plane resilience. Which solution is BEST?

options.text: Multi-AZ worker nodes and pod topology spread

questions.explanation: Spread constraints improve pod availability.

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to backup EKS etcd application state. Which solution is BEST?
questions.explanation: Velero backs up Kubernetes resources and PVs.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would validate recovery procedures regularly. Design B would use cell-based blast isolation for EKS. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Testing confirms RTO/RPO assumptions. Compare with Separate clusters/accounts per cell: Cells contain failures.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would use cell-based blast isolation for EKS. Design B would protect against regional failure. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Cells contain failures. Compare with Multi-Region active-passive or active-active: Multi-Region designs address Regional disasters.
